key_insight: The paper argues that LLM-based theorem provers must evolve from narrow proof solvers into research agents capable of formal reasoning on open-ended frontier mathematics.
engineering_relevance: For AI4Math systems, the next design target is not just tactic generation but robust workflows for exploration, abstraction, tooling, and human-in-the-loop mathematical research.
method: Systematic review of AI4Math progress across datasets, auto-formalization, and proof synthesis
Gap analysis of current systems for frontier research tasks
Road-map proposal covering datasets, relational structure, exploration, tool ecosystems, and human-AI collaboration
evidence_signal: The abstract is a position-paper review rather than a new benchmark study, so its evidence comes from synthesizing prior datasets and system capabilities instead of reporting a single quantitative result.
watch_item: A major implementation risk is that frontier mathematics is under-specified and multi-layered, making formalization, evaluation, and tool integration substantially harder than standard theorem proving.
paper_title: From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier
